name: International Law
introAbove: The international law field offers a diverse range of careers domestically and beyond Australia. With human rights breaches and cross-national conflicts at the cornerstone of the public international law sector, there is a major intersection between public policy and international law. Public international law encompasses diverse practice areas and practices in a vast range of organisations.

orgs.name: Office of International Law
orgs.body: Within the Commonwealth Attorney-General's Department. *(Cross-link to [Government & Public Service](/pathways/government-and-public-service/).)*

orgs.name: United Nations - Young Professionals Programme (YPP)
orgs.body: Recruitment for international civil servants with the UN Secretariat. Entrance examination + structured professional development. P-1 / P-2 entry levels. P-2 requires a bachelor's + 2 years experience OR a master's. Applicants must be 32 years old or younger and a national of a participating UN Member State. Applications open in October via the Inspira portal. https://careers.un.org/young-professionals-programme

orgs.name: United Nations - Volunteer Programme (UNV)
orgs.body: Managed by UNDP, supporting the SDGs. International / national / online volunteering. Age 18+. 6 to 12 months, renewable up to 4 years. No salary, but Volunteer Living Allowance + travel + health insurance + resettlement allowance. Apply via the UNV Unified Volunteering Platform. https://www.unv.org/become-volunteer

orgs.name: International Court of Justice - Internship Programme
orgs.body: Open to students and young professionals. Tasks under Registry officials' supervision; placements across Registry departments and divisions. Limited number per year. Unpaid (own travel, accommodation, medical insurance). English or French required. Apply via the ICJ eRecruitment System only - no hardcopy or email. https://www.icj-cij.org/internships

orgs.name: International Criminal Court - Internship Programme
orgs.body: For final-year university students OR graduates with under 3 years relevant full-time experience. 3 to 6 months full-time at The Hague. Unpaid; own living expenses. English or French. https://www.icc-cpi.int/jobs/internships-and-visiting-professionals

orgs.name: International Criminal Court - Visiting Professionals Programme
orgs.body: Bachelor's + 3 years relevant full-time experience. 1 to 6 months full-time. Generally unpaid, but limited funded positions via the ICC Trust Fund for candidates from developing countries. Same URL as above.

orgs.name: Permanent Court of Arbitration - Internship Programme
orgs.body: 3 months starting in January / April / July / October. Apply 4 months in advance. Final-year law students or recent graduates with strong academics. Australian native English satisfies the language requirement; Arabic, Chinese, Russian, or Spanish advantageous. Self-funded - applicants need adequate financial resources + health insurance for relocating to Vienna or Mauritius. The PCA provides supporting documentation if a travel visa is required. https://pca-cpa.org/en/about/employment/internship-program/

orgs.name: UQ Embassy Internship Program
orgs.body: Strengthens UQ-embassy ties through research, reports, and events. All unpaid. Eligibility: Australian citizen or PR + returning to full-time study + faculty approval (academic credit OR unpaid extracurricular). UQ Law / Political Science students can enrol POLS3801 or POLS7521 for credit. Runs over summer and winter breaks, Canberra-based. https://employability.uq.edu.au/uq-embassy-internship-program
